---
title: "MLSysBook Playground"
subtitle: "Four small games. Each one is a real ML systems concept made playable."
description: "Browser mini-games that turn ML systems concepts — pruning, GPU memory, distributed training stragglers, mixed-precision quantization — into 60-second arcade loops. Part of the MLSysBook ecosystem."
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      - text: |
          <link rel="stylesheet" href="/assets/games/common.css">
---

Four little games. Each one takes about a minute to play and quietly teaches one thing about how ML systems actually work. They share a palette and a vibe with the [ML Systems textbook](/) so they feel like part of the same world.

::: {.mlsp-game-cards}

::: {.mlsp-game-card}
### Pulse Prune
[Shrink a network without breaking it.]{.mlsp-card-tagline}

A neural network on a canvas. You've got 45 seconds to remove most of the weights without crashing accuracy. The faint ones are safe; the bright ones are doing real work.

[▶ Play Pulse Prune](/games/prune.html){.mlsp-card-cta}
:::

::: {.mlsp-game-card}
### Straggler
[Don't be the slow GPU.]{.mlsp-card-tagline}

Eight GPUs in a ring all-reduce. You're one of them. Tap to send your gradient chunk; if you're slow, every other GPU sits idle waiting on you. Every step gets faster. See how many synchronized steps you can complete before you throttle.

[▶ Play Straggler](/games/straggler.html){.mlsp-card-cta}
:::

::: {.mlsp-game-card}
### OOM
[Pack training memory before it overflows.]{.mlsp-card-tagline}

Parameters anchor at the bottom; activations, gradients, and optimizer states fall in their real proportions. Every 3 placements triggers backward (newest activations consumed first), every 6 fires `step()` (gradients used and cleared). Activations dominate — and that's why you'll OOM.

[▶ Play OOM](/games/oom.html){.mlsp-card-cta}
:::

::: {.mlsp-game-card}
### Sharp Shot
[Compress a model before the target blurs.]{.mlsp-card-tagline}

Shoot a downrange target with per-layer precision dials as your sight. Lower precision saves bits but blurs, jitters, or drifts the target depending on which layer you quantized. Seven hits out of ten to ship.

[▶ Play Sharp Shot](/games/quantization.html){.mlsp-card-cta}
:::

:::

---

*MLSysBook Playground is part of [mlsysbook.ai](/). The games share design vocabulary with the textbook — compute blue, data green, error red, MIT accent — so a figure in the book and a game in your browser feel like the same world.*
